qa_pairs.question: What are the specifications of the processors used in the CPU nodes of the Fir cluster, and what is the total core count per node?
qa_pairs.answer: Each CPU node in the Fir cluster features two AMD EPYC 9655 (Zen 5) processors, operating at 2.7 GHz. These processors provide a total of 192 physical cores per node.

qa_pairs.question: What type of architecture do the CPU nodes of the Fir cluster employ, and what are its main characteristics regarding memory and performance?
qa_pairs.answer: The CPU nodes in the Fir cluster are built on a chiplet-based NUMA (Non-Uniform Memory Access) architecture. In this design, each chiplet (CCD) functions as an independent NUMA node, resulting in a non-uniform memory and cache hierarchy where performance is significantly influenced by data locality.
